lessons.title: WhatsApp Business API: Setup and Architecture
lessons.title_es: API de WhatsApp Business: configuración y arquitectura
lessons.description: Configure the WhatsApp Business API end-to-end: Meta account, phone number, webhook, and message templates.
lessons.description_es: Configura la API de WhatsApp Business de principio a fin: cuenta de Meta, número de teléfono, webhook y plantillas de mensajes.
lessons.content:
WhatsApp API Infrastructure Setup

WhatsApp has 2 billion active users and a 98% message open rate — 5-6x higher than email. But access requires the Business API, not the standard WhatsApp Business app. The API unlocks automation, CRM integration, and broadcast messaging at scale. Here's the complete setup process.

Step 1 — Meta Business Suite Setup: Create or verify a Meta Business account at business.facebook.com. The business must be verified (submit legal documents — this takes 1-5 business days). Without verification, you're limited to 250 conversations per day.

Step 2 — Phone Number Registration: You need a phone number that is NOT currently registered to any WhatsApp account. Dedicated business landlines or new SIM cards work. Port the number through a Business Solution Provider (BSP) like Twilio, 360dialog, or directly through Meta Cloud API (now free). The number must be able to receive SMS for verification.

Step 3 — Meta App and Webhook: Create a Meta App in Meta Developer Console. Add the WhatsApp product. Configure a webhook URL (your server endpoint that receives incoming message events). The webhook must respond to the Meta verification challenge with a 200 status and the hub.challenge parameter. Test with Meta's webhook testing tool before going live.

Step 4 — Message Templates: All business-initiated messages (messages you send to users first, or messages sent more than 24 hours after the last user message) must use approved templates. Templates are submitted in Meta Business Manager and typically approved within 24 hours. Template categories: Marketing (promotional), Utility (transactional/updates), and Authentication (OTPs). Utility templates have lower per-message costs than marketing templates.

Step 5 — Phone Number Quality Rating: Meta rates your number quality based on user feedback (blocks, spam reports). Start with low-volume test broadcasts, maintain quality above "Medium" to avoid messaging limits.

  • Use the Meta Cloud API directly instead of a BSP for maximum control and zero per-message markup
  • Register a dedicated number — never use a number that has existing WhatsApp conversations (they are deleted on migration)
  • Build your webhook on a reliable host with 99.9%+ uptime — missed webhooks mean missed messages
  • Submit template variations simultaneously — get 3-4 approved so you can A/B test message copy

lessons.title: Automated Flows: Lead Qualification and Appointment Booking
lessons.title_es: Flujos automatizados: calificación de leads y reserva de citas
lessons.description: Build WhatsApp conversation flows that qualify inbound leads and book appointments without human intervention.
lessons.description_es: Construye flujos de conversación en WhatsApp que califiquen leads entrantes y reserven citas sin intervención humana.
lessons.content:
The WhatsApp Lead Qualification Flow

A well-designed WhatsApp flow qualifies leads, handles objections, and books appointments 24/7 — at a fraction of the cost of a human SDR. The key is designing flows that feel conversational, not robotic. Here's how to build one that converts.

Flow Architecture — The 5-Step Qualification Sequence:

Step 1 — Welcome + Context: Triggered when a new user messages your WhatsApp number (typically from a website CTA, QR code, or ad Click-to-WhatsApp). First message: welcome the user, state who you are and what you help with, and ask the first qualifying question. Keep it under 3 lines. Example: "Hi [Name]! This is the NetWebMedia team. We help US businesses grow with AI marketing. Quick question — what's your biggest marketing challenge right now?"

Step 2 — Problem Identification: Use button-based responses (WhatsApp Interactive Messages) for categorical choices: "Getting more leads / Converting existing traffic / Managing social media / Something else." Buttons increase response rates by 40% vs. free-text prompts. Based on selection, branch to the relevant follow-up question.

Step 3 — Qualification Gatekeeping: Collect two to three key qualifiers: company size, current monthly revenue or ad spend, and urgency/timeline. Use a mix of buttons and free-text. At this point, you should have enough to determine if the lead is sales-qualified.

Step 4 — Booking Integration: If qualified, present a direct booking link via Calendly, Cal.com, or your internal scheduler. Message: "Great — you're a strong fit for what we do. Here's a direct link to book a 30-minute strategy call: [link]. Spots this week: [day] at [time] or [day] at [time]. Which works?" The pre-suggested times reduce decision fatigue and increase booking rates.

Step 5 — Disqualified Lead Nurture: For leads that don't meet qualification thresholds, enroll in a WhatsApp drip sequence (low-touch, 1 message/week) rather than dropping them. Circumstances change — a lead that's too small today may qualify in 6 months.

  • Limit any single flow to 7 messages maximum before a human handoff option — longer flows lose users
  • Always include a human handoff trigger: "Talk to a person" button available at every step
  • Test flows by messaging your own number before going live — what looks clean in the flow builder often reads awkwardly in an actual conversation
  • Connect the flow to your CRM via webhook so every qualified lead is logged automatically with full conversation context

lessons.title: Broadcast Campaigns: Mass Messaging That Converts
lessons.title_es: Campañas de broadcast: mensajes masivos que convierten
lessons.description: Design, segment, and execute WhatsApp broadcast campaigns that drive measurable revenue with sub-1% opt-out rates.
lessons.description_es: Diseña, segmenta y ejecuta campañas de broadcast de WhatsApp que generen ingresos medibles con tasas de baja de menos del 1%.
lessons.content:
The WhatsApp Broadcast Playbook

WhatsApp broadcasts are the highest-converting mass messaging channel available — but they can also permanently damage your number quality if done wrong. This lesson covers the strategy, segmentation, and execution that separates high-performing campaigns from spam reports.

The Opt-In Imperative: Under WhatsApp's terms of service, you can only send marketing messages to users who have explicitly opted in. This is not a legal technicality — users who receive unsolicited WhatsApp messages block and report at high rates, which triggers Meta to restrict your number. Build opt-in at every customer touchpoint: website widget ("Chat on WhatsApp"), post-purchase flow, email CTA, and in-store QR code.

Segmentation Strategy: Never broadcast to your full list. Segment by: purchase history (customers vs. prospects), last interaction date (active last 30 days vs. dormant), and qualification status (SQLs, MQLs, unqualified leads). Each segment gets a different message — one message for all is the fastest way to generate opt-outs.

The High-Converting Broadcast Template:

  • First line: Personalization ("Hi [First Name]") + benefit statement. No generic "We hope this message finds you well."
  • Body (2-3 lines): The offer or value. Be specific. "We just published a free SEO audit tool — no form, instant results." Vague broadcasts don't convert.
  • CTA (1 line): One clear action. "Try it here: [link]" or "Reply YES to get the guide."
  • Image or video: Templates with media get 35-50% higher click rates. Use a 16:9 image at minimum 640px width.

Timing and Frequency: Best send windows: Tuesday-Thursday, 10am-12pm or 6pm-8pm (recipient's time zone). Maximum frequency: 2-3 broadcasts per month per segment. More frequent sends spike opt-out rates. Respect the 24-hour conversation window — if a user replies, you can respond freely for 24 hours without using a template.

  • Always include an opt-out instruction: "Reply STOP to unsubscribe" — this reduces spam reports even if it increases opt-outs
  • Track campaign metrics by template ID in your BSP dashboard: delivery rate, read rate, click rate, and opt-out rate
  • A read rate below 70% signals list quality issues; an opt-out rate above 2% signals message relevance issues
  • Split test message body copy on segments of 500+ users before broadcasting to the full list

lessons.title: CRM Integration and Conversation Intelligence
lessons.title_es: Integración con CRM e inteligencia conversacional
lessons.description: Connect WhatsApp to your CRM for full conversation history, lead scoring, and automated pipeline movement.
lessons.description_es: Conecta WhatsApp a tu CRM para historial completo de conversaciones, puntuación de leads y movimiento automatizado del pipeline.
lessons.content:
WhatsApp-CRM Integration Architecture

An unintegrated WhatsApp setup is a support inbox, not a revenue system. Connecting WhatsApp to your CRM transforms conversations into trackable pipeline — every message becomes a data point that informs lead scoring, triggers automated follow-ups, and drives sales team action.

Integration Architecture:

The standard integration uses webhooks: WhatsApp → Meta Webhook → Your server → CRM API. Every inbound and outbound message fires an event to your webhook. Your server parses the event and writes to the CRM: contact record, conversation log, and any extracted data (answers to qualification questions, appointment booked, etc.).

Data Points to Capture in CRM:

  • Contact phone number (used as unique identifier)
  • First contact source (which campaign, QR code, or CTA triggered the conversation)
  • Qualification answers (mapped to CRM fields: company size, budget, urgency)
  • Conversation sentiment (positive, neutral, objecting) — can be AI-classified per message
  • Stage transitions (prospect → qualified → appointment booked → no-show / completed)
  • Opt-out events (critical for compliance and list hygiene)

Lead Scoring via Conversation Signals: Assign point values to conversation actions. Examples: replied to first message (+5), answered all three qualification questions (+15), clicked booking link (+10), booked appointment (+25), no-showed appointment (−10). A lead with 40+ points routes automatically to a senior sales rep; under 20 points enters the nurture flow.

Automated Pipeline Movement: Configure CRM workflows that trigger on conversation events: appointment booked → move to "Scheduled" pipeline stage + notify assigned rep. No-show after appointment → trigger re-engagement WhatsApp message 2 hours later + create follow-up task for rep. Purchase confirmed → enroll in onboarding WhatsApp sequence + move to "Customer" stage.

  • Use WhatsApp phone number as the universal identifier — it matches across all channels (SMS, voice) better than email
  • Build a conversation archive (store raw message history in your database, not just CRM notes) — it's invaluable for training future automation and resolving disputes
  • Configure business hours in your CRM so automated replies include expected response times for messages received outside hours
  • Audit the integration monthly — webhook failures are silent and can cause days of lost data before anyone notices
